assertions.assertion: Code shows one-step registration: AddServerSideSessions<CustomStore>() with a custom store type parameter
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md shows `AddServerSideSessions<YourCustomStore>()` as the one-step pattern.

assertions.assertion: Code shows two-step registration: AddServerSideSessions() followed by AddServerSideSessionStore<CustomStore>()
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md shows `.AddServerSideSessions().AddServerSideSessionStore<YourCustomStore>()` as the two-step pattern.

assertions.assertion: Response explains that session data contains the serialized AuthenticationTicket protected by Data Protection
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md states 'The session stores the serialized ASP.NET Core AuthenticationTicket... protected using ASP.NET Core's Data Protection API'.

assertions.assertion: Response mentions the three queryable indices: SubjectId (sub), SessionId (sid), and DisplayName
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md has a table showing Subject ID (sub), Session ID (sid), and Display Name as queryable indices.

assertions.assertion: Response notes that UserDisplayNameClaimType is null by default due to PII concerns
assertions.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly states 'UserDisplayNameClaimType is unset (null) by default due to PII concerns'.
